title: Pappobolus lehmannii
taxonomy: Pappobolus lehmannii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to South America. The classification of Pappobolus lehmannii is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, genus Pappobolus, and species P. lehmannii. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification of Pappobolus lehmannii.

intro: Pappobolus lehmannii, also known as Colombian Paw Paw, is a species of plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Pappobolus lehmannii include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions.
